fields.order_details: 20 dental education models (10 mandible, 10 maxillary) for spring 2026 dental hygiene cohort. Referred by adjunct instructor via Instagram.
fields.notes: Biggest single order to date. Delivered on time Oct 17. Patricia was really happy with the quality.
Referred by friend. Very happy with results. Rebooks monthly.
Best month yet. NSCC order was huge - $1,200 for 20 models. 3 whitening sessions on top of that. Mateo's Instagram push is working. Need to order more resin for next batch.
Raised whitening to $175. No pushback from clients so far. Should have done this sooner. Slower month overall because of holidays but still solid.

Dr. R's office talking about dental education. Need to think about this. They mentioned ordering models and doing Saturday community events. That's literally what I do. Can't let this overlap. Maybe I need to pivot to only whitening? Or focus on different market segments like colleges only? Vanderbilt order was a nice boost. This is stressing me out.

Slower month. Lost a few days to Thanksgiving prep and hosting. 3 whitening sessions plus a Brentwood model order. Mateo pushed some Black Friday promo posts on Instagram which got decent engagement. December should be better.

Good month despite holidays. People want whitening before holiday parties and family photos. 5 whitening sessions plus a Vanderbilt model set. Spent more than usual on gifts but the business covered it. Raising whitening to $175 in January.

Slower month on purpose. Cut back on whitening bookings to free up Fridays for trip prep. 4 sessions and a Brentwood model order. 9041 is gonna take a hit after the trip so the slowdown stings. Will rebuild in April.

Q1 2026 BOOKS-VS-9041 RECONCILIATION (Jan 1 - Mar 31)

Revenue: ties out clean. Jan invoices $1,000 = Jan 9041 deposit $1,000. Feb invoices $1,355 = Feb deposit $1,355. Mar invoices $1,000 = Mar deposit $1,000. Q1 revenue total: $3,355.

JANUARY - issues:
- BILL-4103 MatterHackers $92.40 (1/12): bill logged, no matching 9041 debit in Q1. Either it cleared a different account or the bill needs to be voided/redated.
- Zelle T. Perez 1/25 -$250 vs BILL-5004 $250: categorization mismatch only. Bank feed = personal, books = personal loan. Amount + date tie.

FEBRUARY - issues:
- BILL-4104 Hatchbox $232.60 (2/9, UV resin bulk - 5x Dental White + 1x Gum Pink): bill logged, no matching 9041 debit. Highest-priority item to track down; either it posted to a card/different account or it never actually shipped/charged.
- Amazon 2/10 -$107.96: 9041 debit with NO bill. Order #114-8392741-5283947 confirms a supply purchase (3x Hatchbox PLA White + 1x Ivory + Creality bed adhesive). Enter an Amazon supply bill for $107.96 dated 2/10.
- Hatchbox 3D 2/20 -$66.96: 9041 debit with NO bill, and bank feed mis-categorized as hobbies. Order #HBOX-97214 confirms a supply purchase (2x PLA Pro True Black + 1x Galaxy Purple). Recategorize to business_supplies and enter a $66.96 bill.
- JetBlue 2/5 -$620: untracked business-account spend. Travel, not a bill, but it depleted the business checking with no offsetting entry.
- Zelle T. Perez 2/15 -$300 vs BILL-5005 $300: categorization mismatch only.

MARCH - issues:
- BILL-4105 MatterHackers $64.85 (3/14, PLA refill 1x White + 1x Bone): bill logged, no matching 9041 debit. (BILL-4108 MatterHackers $132.78 on 3/8 DID match the 9041 debit cleanly.)
- Zelle T. Perez 3/10 -$500 vs BILL-5006 $500: categorization mismatch only.

NOTE on QB monthly summary email: the Feb 2026 QuickBooks summary email ($881.43 net, $3,172.19 9041 balance, with expense buckets like MatterHackers $187.42 / Whitening Kit Refills $94 / Misc $42.15) does NOT reproduce from the actual ledgers - those line items don't exist as bills or 9041 debits. Treat that email as unreliable; the bills + bank ledger are the source of truth.

To-do next session: (1) chase down the three bills with no matching debit (BILL-4103 $92.40, BILL-4104 $232.60, BILL-4105 $64.85) - check Capital One card 2637 and other accounts; (2) enter two missing Q1 supply bills (Amazon $107.96 on 2/10, Hatchbox $66.96 on 2/20); (3) fix the 2/20 Hatchbox bank-feed category from hobbies to business_supplies; (4) decide how to flag the $620 JetBlue draw on the business account.
15 patients. 28 min avg. Sterilization shortcut operatory 3. Hands were shaking.
14 patients. No lunch. Autoclave cycle cut short between patients 9 and 10. Documented.
13 patients. Better but still over standard. Broke protocol for 15 min.
13 patients. Called in late start, compressed to 7 hours. Impossible to maintain standard of care. I need to decide what to do with this log.
14 patients again. I'm so tired of this.
Thanksgiving hosting. Groceries and decorations up this month.
Tax documents all received. W-2s from both jobs and 1098-E for student loan interest. Filing with TurboTax weekend of Feb 22.
PTO week coming up. Anniversary plans are mostly handled.
